summary:
Claim: A patient with type 2 diabetes diagnosed one year ago without other complications should undergo an ophthalmic examination promptly.  
Evidence: - Updated ADA guidelines recommend dilated eye exams immediately after T2DM diagnosis due to rapid retinopathy progression. - Early detection enables timely treatment, reducing risk of severe visual impairment.  
Answer: A
